description: Diploma frontend — single-run mega-phase replacing P02→P10. Bilingual UA/EN landing site for CARPATHIAN.CONF 2026: i18n shell + 5 pages (Home/Speakers/Schedule/Tickets/Venue) + final a11y/perf pass + Vercel redeploy. CFP form is mock-only (no real Formspree). No custom domain. No diploma write-up. Builds on PHASE-00 baseline + PHASE-01 design system.

userStories.title: i18n shell — next-intl + [locale] segment + Header/Footer + UA/EN parity test
userStories.description: As a bilingual visitor, I want every page available at /uk/* and /en/* with a working locale switcher in the header so that I can read the site in my preferred language. UA is canonical; EN is secondary.
acceptanceCriteria: next-intl wired via src/middleware.ts and src/i18n/{request.ts,routing.ts} with localePrefix: 'always', defaultLocale: 'uk', locales: ['uk','en']
[locale] dynamic segment under src/app/[locale]/ replaces flat src/app/ — root page.tsx redirects / → /uk
src/i18n/locales/uk/common.json + src/i18n/locales/en/common.json with at least: nav (home/speakers/schedule/tickets/venue), footer (copyright, social), locale-switcher labels
src/components/layout/Header.tsx (server component) renders nav links + locale switcher (uses Button primitive from PHASE-01)
src/components/layout/Footer.tsx renders project info + locale-aware links
Locale switcher preserves the current pathname when toggling (uses next-intl Link or useRouter)
tests/unit/i18n/parity.test.ts — fails if any key exists in uk/* but not en/* (or vice versa) across all locale files. Use a recursive deep-key diff
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
pnpm vitest run passes
pnpm build succeeds with both /uk and /en pre-rendering
userStories.notes: ADR-0003 (bilingual UA/EN), ADR-0001 (Next.js 16 App Router). next-intl is already in dependencies per PHASE-00 baseline. Read AGENTS.md + node_modules/next/dist/docs/ for Next 16 specifics before wiring. Existing /dev/components route should still work — keep it under src/app/dev/ (no [locale] prefix).

userStories.title: Home page — hero + marquee + mock CFP form
userStories.description: As a prospective attendee, I want the home page to immediately show me what the conference is, when, where, and let me submit a Call-for-Proposals so that I understand the value proposition and can engage in under 30 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/[locale]/page.tsx renders Hero (event name, dates 14-16 May 2026, location Uzhhorod, primary CTA → CFP) + MarqueeText (rotating tagline) + CFP form section
src/components/home/Hero.tsx uses tokens from PHASE-01, no hardcoded colors/spacing

src/components/home/CfpForm.tsx — react-hook-form + zod schema (name, email, talk title, abstract 50-1000 chars, track select). Client component. Always-mock submit (no real network call): fake 1.2s delay, then success state. No Formspree env var read at all in this story

Form validation: live errors on blur, focus moves to first invalid field on submit attempt, success state replaces form with thank-you message + 'submit another' link, error state (simulated 10% random failure on submit for demo) shows inline error and keeps form data
All form labels are associated with inputs (htmlFor + id), required fields marked aria-required, errors linked via aria-describedby
Reduced-motion respected: marquee static, no entrance animations
Locale-aware: all strings come from src/i18n/locales/{uk,en}/home.json
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
pnpm vitest run passes (form-validation unit tests)
Verify in browser at /uk and /en
userStories.notes: ADR-0004 (no backend) — mock-only is intentional for diploma demo. Use Sheet primitive from PHASE-01 if showing a confirmation modal. Form is securityRelevant because it processes user input even in mock mode (validation logic must be sound).

userStories.title: Home page — below-fold sections (speaker preview, sponsor strip, what-to-expect)
userStories.description: As a visitor scrolling past the hero, I want to see who's speaking, who's sponsoring, and what the conference experience will be like so that I have enough signal to keep reading or buy a ticket.
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/home/SpeakerPreview.tsx — horizontally-scrolling strip of 6 speakers (mock data from src/content/speakers.ts), each card: photo placeholder, name, talk title. Click → /uk/speakers (or /en/speakers)
src/components/home/SponsorStrip.tsx — grayscale logo wall of 8 mock sponsors (text-only logo placeholders, monospace label). Hover reveals color (CSS only, no JS)
src/components/home/WhatToExpect.tsx — 3-column grid (mobile: stacked) explaining tracks/format/community. Uses Tag primitive for category labels + Rule primitive for separators
All sections use Rule primitives between them (per ADR-0002 Swiss discipline — hairlines, not shadows)
Mock data files: src/content/speakers.ts (6 entries), src/content/sponsors.ts (8 entries), all with bilingual fields { uk: '...', en: '...' } where applicable
All strings come from src/i18n/locales/{uk,en}/home.json
Responsive at 375 / 768 / 1280 (no overflow)
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser at /uk and /en
userStories.notes: Mock data lives under src/content/ — these become the source-of-truth for /speakers and /tickets later. Reuse the speaker data on /speakers page (US-004).

userStories.title: Speakers page — filterable grid + Sheet modal for speaker detail
userStories.description: As a visitor curious about the lineup, I want to browse all speakers with optional filtering (by track) and click any speaker to see their full bio in a focus-trapped panel so that I can evaluate the program depth.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/[locale]/speakers/page.tsx — server component, reads src/content/speakers.ts (extend to 12 speakers across 3 tracks)
src/components/speakers/SpeakerGrid.tsx — responsive grid (mobile 1col / tablet 2col / desktop 3col), each card: photo placeholder, name, role/company, talk title, track Tag
src/components/speakers/TrackFilter.tsx — client component, 3 track buttons + 'All'. URL state via search params (?track=design). Active state styled with --color-accent
Click on a speaker card opens Sheet primitive (PHASE-01) with full bio, talk abstract, social links. Focus trapped, Esc closes, returns focus to card
Empty state when no speakers match filter ('No speakers in this track')
All strings + speaker fields are bilingual via src/content/speakers.ts (uk + en fields) and src/i18n/locales/{uk,en}/speakers.json
Sheet content is keyboard-navigable, screen-reader accessible
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser at /uk/speakers and /en/speakers
userStories.notes: Reuses Sheet primitive from US-007 of PHASE-01. Track filter is the diploma's 'filterable list' UX pattern (Ch.2 information architecture).

userStories.title: Schedule page — 3-day tabs + expandable session rows + anchor deep-links
userStories.description: As an attendee planning my conference days, I want to see the schedule split by day with expandable session details and shareable anchor links so that I can plan in advance and send specific sessions to colleagues.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/[locale]/schedule/page.tsx — server component, reads src/content/schedule.ts (3 days × 6-8 sessions per day, with start/end times, speaker refs, room, abstract)
src/components/schedule/DayTabs.tsx — tabs for Day 1 / Day 2 / Day 3 (May 14/15/16). URL state via #day1, #day2, #day3 anchors. Active tab highlighted with --color-accent rule

src/components/schedule/SessionList.tsx — vertical list of sessions for the active day. Each row: time slot (mono), title, speaker name, track tag. Click expands to show abstract (uses Accordion primitive from PHASE-01 — single-open mode)
Each session has a stable ID anchor (#session-{id}) — direct links open the right day tab and expand the session
Time slots in Europe/Kyiv timezone, formatted via Intl.DateTimeFormat with locale
Bilingual: src/content/schedule.ts has uk + en fields, src/i18n/locales/{uk,en}/schedule.json for static labels
Responsive: at 375 the time/title stack vertically
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser at /uk/schedule and /en/schedule, test deep-link to a specific session
userStories.notes: Reuses Accordion primitive from US-006 of PHASE-01. Diploma demonstrates 'tabs + expandable rows + anchor deep-links' (Ch.2 interactions).

userStories.title: Tickets + FAQ page — pricing comparison + sticky CTA + accordion FAQ
userStories.description: As a budget-conscious visitor, I want to compare ticket tiers side-by-side with a clear primary CTA, plus answers to common questions in a collapsible FAQ so that I can decide quickly without scrolling between sections.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/[locale]/tickets/page.tsx — server component, reads src/content/tickets.ts (3 tiers: Student / Standard / Patron — price, features array, recommended flag)

src/components/tickets/PricingTable.tsx — 3-column comparison (mobile: stacked cards). Recommended tier visually emphasized via --color-accent border. Each tier has 'Buy' CTA (mock — opens Sheet with 'Tickets opening soon' message)
Sticky bottom CTA (mobile only) shows 'From $X — Get tickets' that scrolls to pricing on click
src/components/tickets/Faq.tsx — Accordion (PHASE-01 primitive, single-open) with 8 mock Q&As (refunds, dietary needs, recording, code-of-conduct, etc.)
Bilingual: src/content/tickets.ts + src/i18n/locales/{uk,en}/tickets.json
Responsive at 375 / 768 / 1280
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser at /uk/tickets and /en/tickets
userStories.notes: Reuses Accordion + Sheet from PHASE-01. Diploma demonstrates 'comparison table + accordion + sticky CTA' (Ch.2 interactions, Ch.3 conversion).

userStories.title: Venue page — info + gallery lightbox + dl semantics
userStories.description: As a traveling attendee, I want clear information about the venue with photos and travel details (address, transit, accommodation hints) so that I can plan my trip without leaving the site.
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/[locale]/venue/page.tsx — server component, reads src/content/venue.ts (address, coords, capacity, amenities, getting-there sections, 6 mock photo entries)
Address + key facts in a <dl> (definition list) — proper dt/dd semantics

src/components/venue/Gallery.tsx — 3-column grid (mobile: 2col) of 6 photos (use placeholder solid-color blocks or Lorem Picsum URLs with width/height attrs). Click any photo opens Sheet (PHASE-01) with larger view + caption
src/components/venue/GettingThere.tsx — 3 sections (by plane / by train / by car) with bilingual content
Static map fallback: a hardcoded link to 'Open in Google Maps' (no embed, no JS map library — keeps bundle clean and avoids API key concerns)
Bilingual: src/content/venue.ts + src/i18n/locales/{uk,en}/venue.json
All images have explicit width/height (no CLS), loading='lazy' below the fold
Responsive at 375 / 768 / 1280
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
Verify in browser at /uk/venue and /en/venue
userStories.notes: Diploma demonstrates 'lightbox gallery + dl semantics + external links' (Ch.1 information clarity, Ch.2). Skip embedded map — saves bundle + avoids Google Maps API key.

userStories.title: Final a11y + perf pass + production redeploy
userStories.description: As the diploma defender, I want the entire site to meet Lighthouse and axe-core targets across every page × locale, with a fresh production deploy on the canonical Vercel alias so that the committee sees the same quality during defense.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/e2e/diploma-frontend.spec.ts — Playwright spec covering all 5 routes × 2 locales (10 paths) at viewports 375 + 768 + 1280: renders without console errors, no horizontal overflow, axe scan 0 serious/critical violations
Keyboard tour test: Tab through every page hits all interactive elements in logical order, focus rings visible (re-uses pattern from PHASE-01 design-system.spec.ts)
Reduced-motion test: with browser context prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, all marquees/animations are static, transitions instant
Lighthouse CI (.lighthouserc.json updated): assert Performance ≥ 95, Accessibility = 100, Best Practices ≥ 95, SEO = 100 on /uk and /uk/speakers (representative sample)
Bundle size sanity: pnpm build report shows / route ≤ 150kB JS gzipped, other routes ≤ 80kB
Coverage on src/lib/ + src/hooks/ still ≥ 80% (PHASE-01 baseline preserved)
vercel deploy --prod runs successfully; canonical alias https://unik-bice.vercel.app/ serves the new build (HTTP 200, locale redirect / → /uk works)
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes
pnpm lint passes
pnpm build succeeds clean
pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/diploma-frontend.spec.ts passes
userStories.notes: This story closes the loop. If Lighthouse fails on a route, fix in the same iteration (fold images, defer non-critical JS, etc.). For the Vercel deploy step: the project is already linked via .vercel/project.json from PHASE-00; just `vercel deploy --prod` should work.
